L1.area: Model registry and tracking server request handling

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where URIs provided as model artifact sources are not validated for relative path components. An attacker can craft a URI containing directory traversal sequences to escape the intended directory and access or reference arbitrary files on the server's file system. This affects sources using HTTP, HTTPS, and custom artifact protocol schemes.

L3.area: Model version source validation within the tracking server request handlers
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where URIs provided as model version sources are not validated for relative path components. An attacker can craft a source URI containing directory traversal sequences using HTTP, HTTPS, or mlflow-artifacts schemes to escape the intended artifact directory and access arbitrary files on the server. The validation logic for non-local source URIs fails to detect and reject relative path references before processing them.

ground_truth.reason: The _validate_source function does not check for relative path components in non-local source URIs, allowing path traversal attacks via crafted URIs containing '../' sequences to access arbitrary server files.
